<!-- GENERATED by bin/lb-skills-assemble from SKILL.md.tmpl — DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY -->
---
name: knowledge-capture
description: |
  Post-task journal write to the leadbay shared knowledge base. Auto-invoked by
  the Stop hook (always); can also be invoked explicitly. Drops a journal entry
  under journal/YYYY-MM-DD/ AND a compact learning into the local jsonl. Schema-
  validated. Generates a proposed/ entry if a new product concept, mapping, or
  journey was identified.
  Triggers: "save what I learned", "capture this", "log this learning",
  "remember this for next time".
version: 1.0.0
allowed-tools:
  - Bash
  - Read
---

## Preamble (run first)

```bash
# Update check
_UPD=$(~/.leadbay-skills/bin/lb-skills-update-check 2>/dev/null || true)
[ -n "$_UPD" ] && echo "$_UPD" || true
```

If output shows `UPGRADE_AVAILABLE <old> <new>`: tell the user
"Leadbay skills update available: v{old} -> v{new}. Run `~/.leadbay-skills/setup --update`"
and continue with the skill.
## Leadbay Integration Setup (run after preamble)

```bash
_LB_BIN="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." 2>/dev/null && pwd)/bin" 2>/dev/null || _LB_BIN="$HOME/.leadbay-skills/bin"
[ ! -d "$_LB_BIN" ] && _LB_BIN="$HOME/.leadbay-skills/bin"
eval "$("$_LB_BIN/lb-slug" 2>/dev/null)" 2>/dev/null || true
SLUG="${SLUG:-unknown}"
echo "SLUG: $SLUG"
echo "BRANCH: ${BRANCH:-unknown}"
source <("$_LB_BIN/lb-repo-mode" 2>/dev/null) || true
REPO_MODE=${REPO_MODE:-unknown}
echo "REPO_MODE: $REPO_MODE"
# Learnings count
LEADBAY_HOME="${LEADBAY_HOME:-$HOME/.leadbay}"
_LEARN_FILE="$LEADBAY_HOME/projects/$SLUG/learnings.jsonl"
if [ -f "$_LEARN_FILE" ]; then
  _LEARN_COUNT=$(wc -l < "$_LEARN_FILE" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ')
  echo "LEARNINGS: $_LEARN_COUNT entries loaded"
  if [ "$_LEARN_COUNT" -gt 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
    "$_LB_BIN/lb-learnings-search" --limit 3 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
else
  echo "LEARNINGS: 0"
fi
# Check bun availability (needed for learnings commands)
_HAS_BUN=$(command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "yes" || echo "no")
echo "BUN: $_HAS_BUN"
```

If `BUN` is `no`: learnings commands will not work. The diagnostic methodology
still functions fully, but cached knowledge from prior sessions is unavailable.

# /knowledge-capture — Post-task knowledge write

Captures learnings from the just-completed task into the shared knowledge base.
The Stop hook fires this automatically; you can also invoke explicitly to capture
a specific insight mid-session.

## When to invoke explicitly

- The user said "save this" or "remember this".
- You hit a non-obvious gotcha that future agents will hit too.
- You synthesised a mapping (UI ↔ backend) that wasn't documented anywhere.
- You discovered an architectural pattern or pitfall worth promoting.

## What to capture (vs what NOT to)

CAPTURE: things a future agent would have benefited from knowing at the start.
- Conventions you had to infer.
- Behaviour that surprised you or contradicted apparent obvious-ness.
- Hidden contracts between services.
- Pitfalls you stepped on.
- New product concepts or mappings.

DO NOT capture:
- Code patterns already documented in CLAUDE.md or the existing wiki.
- Trivial info ("the file is at src/api.py").
- Anything that's just `git log` away.
- Information you're not confident about.
- Anything containing secrets, PII, or raw customer data.

## How

For each thing worth capturing:

```bash
echo "<one-paragraph insight>" | \
  LB_ACTOR="$(git config user.email 2>/dev/null || whoami)" \
  LB_RUN_ID="${LB_RUN_ID:-$(date +%s)}" \
  LB_SKILL="${LB_SKILL:-knowledge-capture}" \
  "$_LB_BIN/lb-knowledge-write" journal \
    --topic <kebab-name> \
    --type {pattern|pitfall|architecture|operational|preference|tool} \
    --keys <comma,separated,keywords> \
    --confidence <1-10>
```

If you identified a new product concept, mapping, or journey, additionally drop
a `proposed/` entry:

```bash
echo "<concept body>" | \
  "$_LB_BIN/lb-knowledge-write" proposed \
    --concept <kebab-name> \
    --type {concept|mapping|user-intent|journey} \
    --title "<Display Name>" \
    --confidence <1-10>
```

The dream consolidator promotes proposed entries to `wiki/` once they have
cross-source quorum (≥ 2 proposals from distinct slugs).

## Confidence scoring

- 10: user-stated explicitly.
- 8-9: observed and verified in code.
- 6-7: inferred from multiple sources.
- 4-5: educated guess; needs further verification.
- < 4: don't capture.

## After writing

The local timer / wrapper-Action's end-of-run step runs `lb-knowledge-sync` to
push journal entries to `leadbay/knowledge`. The light-dream workflow there
folds them into the wiki within minutes. Your write is visible to the next
agent immediately via `lb-knowledge-find`'s read-time augmentation.
